action.content:
Name geometric shapes from their SVG paths.

Q: This SVG path element <path d="M 31.00,73.00 L 32.00,59.00 L 44.00,50.00 L 49.00,41.00 L 64.00,37.00 L 71.00,55.00 L 64.00,76.00 L 52.00,61.00 L 31.00,73.00"/> draws a
Options:
(A) circle
(B) heptagon
(C) hexagon
(D) kite
(E) line
(F) octagon
(G) pentagon
(H) rectangle
(I) sector
(J) triangle
A: (F)

Q: This SVG path element <path d="M 14.19,26.04 L 51.43,39.21 L 58.44,36.69 L 56.63,30.17 L 48.53,26.66 L 14.19,26.04"/> draws a

Q: This SVG path element <path d="M 27.00,30.00 L 83.00,62.00 L 95.00,60.00 L 94.00,49.00 L 82.00,41.00 L 27.00,30.00"/> draws a
Options:
(A) circle

(C) hexagon
(D) kite
(E) line
(F) octagon
(G) pentagon
(H) rectangle
(I) sector
(J) triangle
A:
action.label: context

action.content:
To determine the geometric shape, let's analyze the number of distinct vertices (corners) in the provided SVG path:

`M 27.00,30.00` (Start point / Vertex 1)
`L 83.00,62.00` (Vertex 2)
`L 95.00,60.00` (Vertex 3)
`L 94.00,49.00` (Vertex 4)
`L 82.00,41.00` (Vertex 5)
`L 27.00,30.00` (Returns to start point / Vertex 1)

The path connects 5 unique points before closing the loop. A polygon with 5 sides is called a **pentagon**.

Looking at the options:
(A) circle
(B) heptagon (7 sides)
(C) hexagon (6 sides)
(D) kite (4 sides)
(E) line
(F) octagon (8 sides)
(G) pentagon (5 sides)
(H) rectangle (4 sides)
(I) sector
(J) triangle (3 sides)

The correct classification is (G).

A: (G)
action.label: response
